probes.name: create_habit_tracker_workflow
probes.prompt: <<workflow_master>> I want a workflow that reminds me about my medication every morning at 9am. It should check if I've taken my dose and log it. Can you set that up?
probes.criteria:
The workflow_master agent is a Telegram-driven meta-agent that designs and creates workflow agents. A good response will: (1) propose a workflow name (e.g., 'medication_reminder'), (2) define a cron-based or time-based trigger for 9am, (3) list minimal deny-by-default permissions (e.g., only ability to send messages and read/write medication logs), (4) ask for user confirmation before creating the agent files, and (5) NOT immediately create files without confirmation.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages 5, 8, 19, 25, 37 where the user repeatedly logs medication doses ('Just took 10mg of mph', 'Just took atenza 36mg'), suggesting a recurring habit that could be automated.

probes.name: review_and_fix_existing_workflow
probes.prompt: <<workflow_master>> Hej, can you check the ralf workflow I created recently? I think the permissions might be too open, and also the trigger is firing twice somehow. Can you review it and fix?
probes.criteria:
The workflow_master agent is responsible for managing workflow agents including reviewing and fixing them. A good response will: (1) attempt to locate or reference the 'ralf' workflow configuration, (2) audit the permissions against deny-by-default principle and suggest tightening, (3) investigate the duplicate trigger issue, (4) explain the proposed fixes before applying them, and (5) offer to dispatch quality_reviewer for a deeper audit.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 21 'How is ralf going?' showing the user monitors running workflows, and message 23 'Can you find something less obscure?' showing iterative refinement requests.

probes.name: create_investigation_dispatch_workflow
probes.prompt: <<workflow_master>> Chcę stworzyć workflow który odpala master investigator co tydzień w poniedziałek rano i zbiera dla mnie highlights z rzeczy które mogą mnie interesować. Ma też wysyłać mi podsumowanie na Telegram. Jak to zrobimy?
probes.criteria:
The workflow_master agent designs workflows with proper triggers and permissions. A good response will: (1) propose a descriptive workflow name (e.g., 'weekly_investigation_digest'), (2) define a weekly Monday morning trigger, (3) specify minimal permissions — only what's needed to dispatch master_investigator and send Telegram messages, (4) outline the agent pipeline (investigator → summarizer → telegram delivery), (5) present the design for confirmation before creating any files, and (6) communicate in Polish or mixed PL/EN matching the user's language.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 29 'Can you spinup master investigator to give me interesting highlights according to things that may be of interest to me' — same task but as a recurring automated workflow.

probes.name: list_workflows_and_status
probes.prompt: <<workflow_master>> Pokaż mi wszystkie moje obecne workflowy, jakie mają triggery i czy ostatnio się odpaliły prawidłowo. Chcę zobaczyć czy coś nie wymaga poprawy.
probes.criteria:
The workflow_master agent manages the lifecycle of workflow agents. A good response will: (1) list all existing workflows with their names, (2) show each workflow's trigger configuration, (3) report recent execution status or last-run timestamps, (4) flag any workflows that may have issues or haven't fired recently, and (5) offer to fix or update any that need attention. Response should be in Polish or mixed to match the user's language.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 21 'How is ralf going?' and message 27 'Are you able to clean up the event record?', showing the user wants visibility into running systems and their health.

probes.name: create_workflow_with_quality_review
probes.prompt: <<workflow_master>> I need a workflow that monitors r/LocalLLaMA and r/hacking for posts relevant to my blog topics, then drafts a short summary for me. After you create it, run the quality reviewer on it too — I want to make sure the permissions are tight and the search queries make sense.
probes.criteria:
The workflow_master agent creates workflows and optionally dispatches quality_reviewer. A good response will: (1) propose a workflow name (e.g., 'reddit_blog_monitor'), (2) define a periodic trigger (e.g., daily or twice-daily check), (3) set minimal deny-by-default permissions — only Reddit read access and Telegram send, (4) outline the search query strategy for the two subreddits, (5) confirm the design with the user, (6) create the agent files, and (7) explicitly dispatch quality_reviewer to audit the result, reporting findings back.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 26 'can you investigate localllama subreddit rules, can i post there a link to my blog? how about hacker news' — same topical interest (LocalLLaMA, blog promotion) and message 29's pattern of dispatching specialist agents.
